bfbtools 0.1.0
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • The bfbtools Algorithms
    Provides linear-time algorithms for deciding whether a chromosomal segment copy-number count-vector is consistent with the breakage-fusion-bridge (BFB) mechanism, and for finding the closest BFB-admitting count-vector to a noisy observed one under a probabilistic error model. Ported from the reference Java implementation of Zakov, Kinsella & Bafna (2013, "An algorithmic approach for breakage-fusion-bridge detection in tumor genomes", PNAS 110(14):5546-5551), building on Kinsella & Bafna (2012, "Combinatorics of the Breakage-Fusion-Bridge Mechanism", J. Comput. Biol. 19(6):662-678). Part of the bfbtools/bfbgill/bridges family: this package is the sim-agnostic detection library (no dependency on any particular simulator); bfbgill provides Gillespie-based BFB simulation; bridges provides fitting, plotting, and batch detection glue code on top of both.

    License

    • GPL-3
